title: fuirena simplex var. simplex
intro: Fuirena simplex var. simplex, also known as the Least Club Rush, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Fuirena simplex var. simplex include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be made by boiling the leaves and rhizomes in water.
